rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Banned words — grep zero-tolerance.
Ritual / practice (as noun) / ceremony — hard ban
No stale markers, no "fixed" retrospectives, no session biography.
Register match.
Rough-cut drafts for articles.
Credit the original when paraphrasing a named technique.
Always "you", never "the student"; teacher framing stays out of body.
Don't smuggle terms of art from maintainer into body.
Over-hedge detector.
Verbatim-frame-cite in reference artefact.
Cap practitioner attribution at one student-side mention per module.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 10: "watch Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen, [You're the Bread in the AI Sandwich]...; read Kieran Klaassen, [Compound Engineering...]" AND 90: "**Read — Kieran Klaassen, [My AI Had Already Fixed the Code...]..."  — Klaassen named twice in body prose across two sections of same module file.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Consolidate attribution to one well-placed mention; prefer the section where the Klaassen move is operationally run (the pre-read sections both name him — keep the pre-reads before Module 6 cite at line 90 and drop the inline name at line 10, referring to the piece by title only).

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Punchy framing keeps its punch; carve-out lives downstream.
Value-prop / positioning framing belongs in sales copy + project-level strategy docs, NOT in student-facing curriculum body.
Watch out for planting jargon too early.
Audit the closer when trimming a body-prose forcing function.
No combative verbs about the agent in body prose.
Aspirational destination, never corrective gap.
Earn every technical term on first use.
Business-audience word ban (Bootstrap default).
Memory vs context — use deliberately.
Golden path only in the body.
Trust Claude's defaults for structure.
Q1/Q2/Q3 retro interview is banned at every module.
Don't smuggle another module's voice.
Don't ask the student to manually diff or spot-check Claude's output as error-catching.
Student moves stay in conversation with the agent.
Specify which CLAUDE.md layer — every time.
No em-dashes. Simple sentences.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 22: "Module 5's work — diagnosis, verifier, reference, plan.md, and the re-send — runs in the worktree"; 24: "AE101's default is to copy them in once at fork time — your Module 1 and Module 3 evidence rides forward"; 78: "the agent isn't the agent any more — that's a result worth reading"; 90: "**Read — Kieran Klaassen, [My AI Had Already Fixed the Code Before I Saw It]"
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Split each em-dash into two sentences or replace with colon/comma: e.g. line 78 'the agent isn't the agent any more. That's a result worth reading.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Section headers use command verbs when the section is about doing something.
rules_evaluated.evidence: 86: "## Pre-reads before Module 6" — noun-phrase header for an action section (student reads). Canonical section names are exempt; this is a non-canonical section header.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Rename to "## Read before Module 6" to lead with verb.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Inline links use the target's title, never its filename.
Disambiguate shared-word collisions with Claude Code primitives on first student-authored use.
LLM-vs-agent-vs-Claude vocabulary discipline.
No time-of-day anchors in body prose.
Reflection beats stay invisible in the flow.
No meta-ask when the student has already decided.
.md is the default file type for student artifacts across the curriculum.
Cut NVA: true but not moving the student forward.
No narrating the student's past or present state
rules_evaluated.evidence: 44: "You sent off an un-packaged run at the close of Module 4 and read the pre-read. You walk in with the artefact in front of you." — narrates student's past action and current state.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Sutherland-flip: drop the narration; open directly with what's in front of them. e.g. 'The Module 4 artefact is the source material. The in-room lecture below opens the session.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: No overclaim of student verification or coverage
